description: Francis Picabia was at the center of Dada’s more celebrated activities and served as its public face between 1917 and October 1924. He regularly exhibited at the official Paris salons but taunted them with his use of highly unconventional materials. Here, he augmented the oil paint on canvas with wooden matchsticks, hairpins, coins, leather hair rollers, and string to form the features of the woman's face.
title: Untitled (Match-Woman I)
